data.body:
SAME-CLASS GAP SWEEP (user-prompted, Step-7). Jira-specific reconciler gaps:
- 🔴 P1 WAS INCOMPLETE (found + fixed): production comment dedup goes through the BULK get_comment_map (/search/jql fields=[comment]) which CAPS embedded comments at 20/issue while reporting true total (live-confirmed: 20 of 5000 for REB-155). _diff_comments snapshot-carried branch trusted the truncated field (no guard) -> re-posts continue for the 13 tickets. The per-ticket get_comments pagination fix only covered the FALLBACK path. FIX: truncation guard in _diff_comments (outbound_comments.py) — when total > len(embedded) and a client is available, re-fetch the full paginated set. RED->GREEN tests assert the re-fetch fires (client.calls==1) + no re-fetch when complete.
- ✅ P2 complete: get_issuelinks_map does NOT truncate (issuelinks is a flat array in Jira search, not a paged sub-resource — live-checked).
- ✅ search_issues safe: acli search --paginate returns ONE merged array (not concatenated per-page objects like comment list). Different acli output shape per subcommand.
- ⚠️ Residual (for test-hardening/P3): (a) outbound-write direction (set_relationship) has NO deterministic absolute-direction test — only external — despite 3 historical re-inversions; (b) audit other acli exit-0-on-failure mutations.
Also fixed: tests/scripts/reducer/test_managed_refs.py had another reversed-convention fixture (inwardIssue+blocks) that my removal-path fix correctly broke — CI caught it because I'd only run the reconciler unit subset locally. LESSON: run the FULL default suite (make test) before every push.
STATUS: re-stacked (truncation guard -> P1/770; managed_refs -> P2/769). FULL default suite GREEN (6373 passed, 0 failed, minus 3 local-env config flakes). Re-pushed 769+770. CI re-running.
